concepts.name: Scenario Facilitation
concepts.definition: The design and guidance of conversations that surface assumptions, manage uncertainty, balance voices, and move groups from discomfort to concrete commitments.

concepts.name: Human Perception and Fear
concepts.definition: Scenario work deals with mental models, anxiety, defensive routines, and the way people perceive ambiguity; poor facilitation can trigger avoidance rather than learning.

concepts.name: Social Intelligence
concepts.definition: The facilitator's ability to read group dynamics, power, emotion, and interaction patterns so scenario work remains productive.

arguments.claim: Scenario planning lives or dies on facilitation quality.
arguments.evidence: The same method can deepen thinking or become theater depending on how assumptions, power dynamics, dominant voices, and discomfort are handled.

arguments.claim: Facilitators must hold productive discomfort without creating paralysis.
arguments.evidence: Scenarios intentionally challenge certainty; groups need enough tension to rethink assumptions but enough safety to commit to action.

cases.name: Pre-workshop interviews
cases.context: Facilitators interview participants before workshops to surface assumptions, language, concerns, and mental models.
cases.illustrates: Process design begins before the room; facilitation is diagnostic as well as conversational.
cases.lesson: Design the workshop around the group's real assumptions and power dynamics.

logicChains.description: Interview stakeholders → surface assumptions and fears → design exercises → manage voices and bias → build scenarios → close with commitments and owners.
logicChains.sourceHint: Managing scenario projects and human perceptions chapters.

terminology.definition: A person's internal representation of how the world works, which scenario planning seeks to challenge and expand.
A participant whose position or style can narrow group thinking if not managed.
A concrete action or ownership decision that closes the workshop with accountability.

nuance: Avoiding discomfort produces shallow scenarios; unmanaged discomfort produces anxiety and resistance.
Facilitation is not neutral logistics; it is central to whether scenario planning changes thinking.
